title: Feature Engineering and Selection
summary: In industry, feature engineering often matters more than model choice.
ml_connection: Production ML spends 80% of time on feature engineering. Bad features → bad model regardless of algorithm.
exercises: Given a dataset with missing values, compare mean/median/mode imputation vs model-based.
Implement target encoding and show why it causes data leakage without proper cross-encoding.
Use Lasso to perform feature selection on a high-dimensional dataset.
Create polynomial features and show when they help vs overfit.
code_example:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler, PolynomialFeatures
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline

# Standard preprocessing pipeline
pipeline = Pipeline([
    ('imputer', SimpleImputer(strategy='median')),
    ('scaler', StandardScaler()),
    ('poly', PolynomialFeatures(degree=2, include_bias=False))
])

X_transformed = pipeline.fit_transform(X_train)
thinking_questions: What is data leakage and how does target encoding cause it?
Why standardize before SVM and neural networks but not before trees?
